tax: Placed at class rank, Air Elementals is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a pure air-element body, moving, settled, or charged, animated by air-pressure. A new order belongs here only if it shows a distinct air-state body-plan with its own source and field signs.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a dust-laden column, an invisible flame-killing pool, an arc-veined cloud, or a clinging glowing mote. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — spiralling dust, a guttering flame, arc-scorch, or a fizzing static residue — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Air Elementals occupies shafts, draught galleries, low dips, charged shafts, and dry static galleries. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where air-pressure animates a draught, foul-gas pocket, charged pocket, or surface static and recur with the draught resumes or the gas, charge, or static rebuilds, sustaining themselves on air-pressure, channelled draught, foul gas, or electrical charge.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: members hide as ordinary air, mist, or a faint glow and rouse to intrusion, flinging, suffocating, striking, or shocking. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a bounded-air body, immunity to physical attack, and an enclosure, ventilation, grounding, or damp failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each order and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: flinging, suffocation, lightning, and contact-shock. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Air Elementals matters because they make ledges, low dips, and charged galleries lethal in invisible ways. It lets survey reports sort air hazards by state, so a crew knows whether to double-clip, test-flame, stow metal, or dry-wax their lines without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Air Elementals is the class-rank grouping of true elementals composed of the air element, from gust to storm within the True Elementals. Its members share a body of bounded air — moving, settled, or charged — drawn from draught, seep, or storm rather than bred, and divide below into order groups by whether the air is uncharged (Aeolian) or charged (Fulgurant). Air is the hardest elemental class to see and so the one that kills the unwary scout, betrayed only by dust, dying flame, or crawling arcs.
